description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that defines a single "build" stage and includes many jobs named gentoo_musl_llvm_builder, ubuntu_curr_builder, ubuntu_curr_builder_arm32, ubuntu_curr_builder_arm64, basic_syntax_check_clang, build_gentoo_musl_llvm_release, build_macos_arm, build_macos_intel, build_ubuntu_2004_release, build_ubuntu_2204_release, build_ubuntu_2404_release, build_ubuntu_arm32_clang19_release, build_ubuntu_arm32_gcc13_release, build_ubuntu_arm32_gcc13_release_lto_full, build_ubuntu_arm32_gcc13_release_lto_thin, build_ubuntu_arm64_clang19_release, build_ubuntu_arm64_gcc13_release, build_ubuntu_arm64_gcc13_release_lto_full, build_ubuntu_arm64_gcc13_release_lto_thin, build_ubuntu_clang18_release, build_ubuntu_clang18_release_lto_full, build_ubuntu_clang18_release_lto_thin, etc., all running in the build stage. Each job uses a specific Docker image from the registry.gitlab.com/synsense-sys-int/inivation/infra/docker-files repository (e.g., gentoo_musl_llvm:latest, ubuntu:rolling, ubuntu:20.04, ubuntu:22.04, ubuntu:24.04, ubuntu:rolling_arm32, ubuntu:rolling_arm64, macOS homebrew paths) and shares a ccache cache keyed by ${CI_JOB_NAME}-${CI_COMMIT_REF_SLUG} with pull‑push policy. All jobs have identical artifacts configuration that always uploads the build/failed_tests/* directory and build/test-report.xml as a JUnit report, and they all apply the same rules: run on merge‑request pipelines, branch pipelines, or tag pipelines but never on manual web pipelines when $RELEASE is set. The script for most Linux jobs starts with the reusable step rm -Rf build; mkdir build; cd build, then runs cmake with appropriate flags (e.g., -DCMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX=/usr, optional -DUSE_CLANG_LIBCPP=ON, -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release), followed by make -j4 -s and optionally make tests TESTS=minimal or TESTS=noperf; macOS jobs additionally install the dv‑python package via pip, set PLATFORM, run ../packaging/macosx/package_deps.sh, zip the result and upload it to an S3 bucket using aws s3 cp. Variables such as CC, CXX, CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE, CFLAGS, CXXFLAGS, UNW_ARM_UNWIND_METHOD, and PATH are set per job to select clang‑18/19 or gcc‑13 and to enable LTO variants, and the basic_syntax_check_clang job runs clang++ -std=c++20 -O2 -pipe -fsyntax-only on every header file found under include/.
